Extension prototype false positives: DATA-001/SEC-080 are the documented hardcoded demo vault (demo emails + placeholder passwords) in extension/popup.js — README states production uses a native-messaging bridge and never bundles secrets; SEC-163 matches are CSS selector strings in content.js (input[autocomplete=current-password], querySelector), not secrets.

Web-vault false positives: app/src/lib/seed.ts is the documented first-run DEMO vault (demo emails, placeholder passwords, demo TOTP secrets) — sealed by real crypto on first unlock, nothing special-cased; AddItemDialog.vue:80 is an email input placeholder; ItemDetail.vue:96 renders the user's own decrypted password on reveal (login.password), not a hardcoded secret.

Accepted+tracked: gtk-rs 0.18 / unic / proc-macro-error advisory set (RUSTSEC-2024-0411..0420, 0370, 0429; 2025-0075..0100; GHSA-wrw7-89jp-8q8g) pulled ONLY transitively by Tauri v2's Linux GTK GUI backend, in the DETACHED app/src-tauri desktop shell (its own workspace, not built by the core product/server). All are unmaintained/unsound advisories, not remote-exploitable; not on the macOS runtime path (WebKit backend). No core crate (passbook/sync/crypto/broker) or the sync-server is affected. Revisit when Tauri bumps its gtk-rs pins.

All in the isolated demo/ environment (never shipped to production): seed.sh uses example.com demo emails; docker-compose PROCTOR_SYNC_ADDR 0.0.0.0 is bind-all (not a public IP); the demo Dockerfile's rust/debian base images are unpinned — digest-pinning is left to a real deployment to keep the throwaway demo simple.
